heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 19 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 6 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwaves in progress.  So far, 6 heatwavess have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (199°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 3.5°C), outside temperature (down 2.9°C), and feels like temperature (down 2.2°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in pressure (down 5 mbar) and cloud levels (down 160 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in outside temperature (up 9.6°C), inside temperature (up 7.5°C), and inside humidity (up 9%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 159.6m higher than yesterday, 334.1m higher than the past week, 378.5m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 623m to a maximum of 1932m, averaging 1415.6m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1081.5m and 1037.1m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight.

summary.summary: Inside; relatively cool weather. Outside; subzero temperatures are forecasted. Also, dry and less humid conditions and winds will be practically absent. Drizzle or light rain possible, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and overcast conditions will limit sunlight. Changeable conditions.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-0.7°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
